primary_id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_1

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_1
elements.text: Compute the order of 2 with respect to the prime moduli 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_2
elements.text: Compute the order of 10 with respect to the modulus 7.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_3
elements.text: Let ri denote the least nonnegative residue of 10* (mod 7). Compute Ti for i = 1,..., 6. Compute the decimal expansion of the fraction 1/7 without using a calculator. Can you find where the numbers ri,..., r6 appear in the process of dividing 7 into 1?

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_4
elements.text: Compute the order of 10 modulo 13. Compute the period of the fraction 1/13.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_5
elements.text: Let p be prime and a an integer not divisible by p. Prove that if a?" = -1 (mod p), then a has order 2n+1 modulo p.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_6
elements.text: Let m be a positive integer not divisible by 2 or 5. Prove that the decimal expansion of the fraction 1/m is periodic with period equal to the order of 10 modulo m.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_7
elements.text: Prove that the decimal expansion of 1/m is finite if and only if the prime divisors of m are 2 and 5.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_8
elements.text: Prove that 10 has order 22 modulo 23. Deduce that the decimal expansion of 1/23 has period 22.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_9
elements.text: Prove that if p is a prime number congruent to 1 modulo 4, then there exists an integer x such that x? = -1 (mod p).

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_10

docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_13
elements.text: Prove that if n ≥ 2, then 2" - 1 is not divisible by n.

elements.id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0085_14
elements.text: Hint: Let p be the smallest prime that divides n. Consider the congruence 2" = 1 (mod p).
